conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm an AI mental health assistant — everything we discuss here is completely confidential, so please feel comfortable. How was your drive over?
Oh wow, okay, an AI — I wasn't sure what to expect honestly, but knowing it stays between us does help. The drive was a bit stressful actually.
Sorry to hear that the drive was stressful. Can you tell me a little bit about what's been going on in your life lately?
Honestly? It's just been really tough getting out of bed in the morning. I mean, I'm a social worker and I love my job, but it feels like this exhaustion has taken over.
I understand how that can be really debilitating. Can you tell me more about what your typical day looks like?
Well, I usually get up around 7 am and then spend an hour or so trying to wake myself up before work. Sometimes I'll re-read emails or scroll through social media to get my brain going.
That sounds like a struggle. What's been going on at work that might be contributing to these feelings?
Honestly, just feeling overwhelmed with cases and paperwork. It feels like I'm drowning in tasks.
I see. Have you had any support from your colleagues or supervisors about this?
Not really. They're all super busy too, so it's hard to get anyone to listen.
That can be frustrating. How have you been handling sleep lately? Are you getting enough rest?
Honestly, I'm exhausted. I mean, I might get 4-5 hours of sleep some nights and then wake up feeling like I've been hit by a truck.
I'm so sorry to hear that. Have you noticed any patterns or triggers that might be affecting your sleep?
Yeah, I think it's just stress in general. Sometimes I'll stay up late watching TV or browsing the internet because my brain won't shut off.
Okay, got it. And what about energy levels during the day? Are you able to tackle tasks without feeling drained?
Honestly, no. I feel like I'm stuck in a rut and just want to go back to bed.
That sounds really tough. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or interest in activities that you used to enjoy?
Yeah, my appetite is all over the place. Some days I'll be starving and eat everything in sight, while other days I won't even want to get out of bed.
That sounds really challenging. What about hobbies or things you used to enjoy? Have you had any time for those lately?
Honestly, not really. It's hard to muster up the energy to do anything fun when you're feeling so low.
Okay, I get it. What about concentration and decision-making? Are you finding that you're able-bodied or having trouble focusing?
Yeah, it's like my brain is a foggy mess. I'll try to make decisions, but they just feel wrong or unclear.

That sounds really frustrating. Can you tell me more about what's been going on in your relationships lately? Are there any significant changes or stressors that might be contributing to these feelings?
Honestly, my social life is pretty non-existent right now. I've cancelled plans with friends and stopped attending community events.
Okay, got it. What about recent stressful life events? Has anything big changed in the past few months?
Yeah, actually. My grandma passed away recently, and that's been really tough for me.
I'm so sorry to hear that. It can be really difficult to process those kinds of losses. Have you had any support or resources that have helped you cope with your grief?
Not really. Just my mom, who's been great, but she's also going through her own stuff right now.
I see. What do you think might help you feel more grounded and in control of your life right now?
Honestly? I'm not sure. Maybe just having someone to talk to without judgment would be a start.
That sounds like a great place to start. Would it be helpful for us to set some small, achievable goals for you to work towards in the next week or so?
Yeah, that might be nice actually.
Okay, let's do that. What are some things you think would make your life feel more enjoyable or fulfilling right now?
Honestly? Just being able to get out of bed without feeling like I'm going to cry the whole time.
That sounds like a great place to start. Let's work on building up those small, achievable goals and see where that takes us in the next few sessions.
